title: Introduction to Block Coding
description: Learn the basics of block-based programming with interactive visual blocks.
fullDescription: This foundational lesson introduces students to the world of coding using visual blocks. Students will learn how code works and create simple programs using drag-and-drop blocks.

teacherInstructions.setup: Open the block coding tool on all devices and place printed command cards at each table for quick reference.
steps: Introduce coding as giving clear instructions to a computer (4 min).
Demonstrate dragging and snapping blocks to create a short sequence (5 min).
Let students recreate the demo sequence in pairs (6 min).
Run student sequences and discuss what worked and what failed (5 min).
discussionPrompts: What makes instructions easy for a computer to follow?
What happened when blocks were in the wrong order?
How is block coding similar to giving directions to a friend?
tips: Model mistakes on purpose so students learn debugging from the start.
Use pair work to reduce frustration during first interactions.
Celebrate tiny wins to build confidence early in the course.

teacherGuide: Start by explaining what coding is and why it's important. Demonstrate how block-based coding works by dragging blocks, then ask students to try it themselves. Monitor progress and provide hints when needed. Celebrate successes to build confidence.

studentExercises.title: Drag Your First Block
studentExercises.description: Drag a simple block and see what happens

studentExercises.title: Create a Simple Sequence
studentExercises.description: Stack 3 blocks in order to create a sequence

studentExercises.title: Build Your First Program
studentExercises.description: Create a program that draws a shape
